title: Health & Wellness Seminar - Leading the Way in Neuroscience and Neurosurgical Care at NGHS
description: Michael Glanzman, Executive Director of Neurosciences for Northeast Georgia Health System and Dr. Zain Boghani, neurosurgeon with Northeast Georgia Physicians Group will offer a high-level overview of the Neuroscience service line at NGHS, featuring its comprehensive clinical programs and specialized teams. They will then dive deeper into the Neurosurgery program, exploring surgical treatment options and the seamless, coordinated care that defines the patient experience. Together, they will highlight how their multidisciplinary teams work together to serve and support communities throughout the region. Michael Glanzman, Executive Director of Neurosciences for Northeast Georgia Health System Zain Boghani, MD, neurosurgeon with Northeast Georgia Physicians Group If you would like to attend on zoom, please register via the registration button. An email will be sent prior to the seminar with a zoom link. 230 E.E. Butler Parkway Gainesville, GA 30501 | Phone: (770) 532-6206

title: Small Business Seminar - Future-Ready Hall County
description: Tuesday, April 14th Networking 8:15 - 8:30 AM Seminar 8:30 - 9:30 AM Future-Ready Hall County helps small business owners move from AI curiosity to AI confidence. In this engaging session, Renee Hayes and Tai Anderson introduce the basics of AI and demonstrate accessible tools that can drive growth, increase efficiency, and sharpen decision-making. Walk away with practical steps to future-proof your North Georgia business. Renee Hayes, MBA, is the Executive Director of Evergreen Learning at the University of North Georgia, where she led the successful launch of the university’s newest venture into lifelong learning & Tai Anderson, a unique voice in the speaking world, known for his innovative fusion of musical artistry and marketing acumen as a founding member of the Grammy-winning band Third Day. As Creative Director for Supreme Lending Southeast and an Evergreen Learning Instructor at the University of North Georgia, Tai brings those lessons from the stage into the business world. Through Continuing Education courses and keynote presentations, he helps professionals understand the power of brand storytelling, fan-driven marketing, and meaningful audience engagement 8:15 - 8:30 AM

title: South Hall Business Coalition - King's Hawaiian
location: 00 AM 5425 Aloha Way Flowery Branch,
description: King's Hawaiian Tuesday, April 21 9:00 - 10:00 AM 5425 Aloha Way Flowery Branch, GA 30542 Join us for an update and tour of the King's Hawaiian Bakery in South Hall County, where their flagship product, Hawaiian sweet bread, is produced. Tour attendees will see a state-of-the-art facility and learn what it takes to produce the irresistible sweet rolls from start to finish. Founded in 1955, the company has grown from a small bakery operation in Hawaii, to a distribution company selling bread, rolls, and pretzels bites nationwide. The family-owned food company expanded its Oakwood facility, creating more than 160 new jobs. This new 150,000-square-foot bakery is located across the street from the King's Hawaiian facility in Oakwood South Industrial Park. King's Hawaiian opened in Oakwood in 2010, and today provides over 800 jobs for the Hall County community. **SOLD OUT, please contact Ellie Cardin ecardin@ghcc.com to be added to waitlist* $8.00 Member $15.00 Non-Member Light breakfast included 230 E.E. Butler Parkway Gainesville, GA 30501 | Phone: (770) 532-6206

title: Small Business Seminar - Why Managers Often Fail to Lead - And How Not to Be One of Them
description: Tuesday, May 5th Networking 8:15 - 8:30 AM Seminar 8:30 - 9:30 AM Most organizations create a culture where personnel and staff are effective in accomplishing the organizational mission. These same individuals, however, are often ineffective when promoted to supervisors and managers in their practice of recognized leadership skills such as communication and dealing with problems and problem employees and managers. Why do effective individuals become ineffective at practicing what we all know as leadership attitudes and behaviors? This program examines factors such as training, ineffective modeling, loss of relational connection and accountability- and how to turn these factors around to cultivate effective leaders. Jack Enter has been associated with leadership development and training for 42 years. He has lectured throughout the United States and a dozen other countries and authored two books on leadership. He has served in leadership positions in law enforcement, the military, and in academia. Much of his presentation is based on information that attendees have shared informally during the breaks in his classes. Most importantly, he has done a dozen climate surveys in which he in

title: Health & Wellness Seminar - Assistive Technology
description: The world around us is always inventing! Emily Major with LegacyLink stays up to date with the latest technologies that may be able to assist those with disabilities, limitations, or anyone who is simply curious as to what possibilities are out there. Technology does not have to be “high-tech” either, it could be as simple as a magnifying glass or an adaptive shoelace. You never know what someone will invent next! Emily has worked at Legacy Link as their Program Monitor and Assistive Technology Coordinator for 6 years. She is passionate about helping adults age in place, advocates for a healthy aging population, and encourages clients to voice their concerns to promote positive change. 230 E.E. Butler Parkway Gainesville, GA 30501 | Phone: (770) 532-6206

title: 118th Annual Gala
location: 3105 Clarks Bridge Rd Gainesville ,
description: A Celebration of Legacy & Leadership Presented by the Board of Directors of the Greater Hall Chamber of Commerce 118th Annual Meeting & Gala Thursday, May 7, 2026 6:00 p.m. - 9:00 p.m. The Boathouse at Lake Lanier Olympic Park We invite you to a lakeside evening of celebration marking the Chamber’s 118th year and honoring the leaders and businesses that shape our community. Set against the beautiful backdrop of Lake Lanier, this signature event features the presentation of Annual Business Awards, recognizing outstanding small and family-owned businesses, along with individual honors for exceptional leadership and distinguished service. The evening will also include the Passing of the Gavel, as we welcome our incoming executive leadership and usher in a new year for the Greater Hall Chamber. title: Health & Wellness Seminar - Regenerative Medicine in the 21st Century
description: Join Dr. Harlan South for a seminar on regenerative medicine and how Anewyou Aesthetics can help you reach your health goals. Dr. South’s passion for regenerative medicines stems from his own personal health struggles. After experiencing firsthand how regenerative therapies transformed his life, he dedicated his career to helping others achieve their fullest potential. This personal connection drives his commitment to delivering innovative, personalized care that truly changes lives. Expect the discussion of the Regenerative Medicine services to include bio-identical hormones, peptides, stem cells, and exosomes as well as how they inter-relate and optimize our total health and wellness. Dr. Harlan L. South 230 E.E. Butler Parkway Gainesville, GA 30501 | Phone: (770) 532-6206

title: Small Business Seminar - Culture as a Legacy: Navigating Succession Planning with an Ownership Mindset
description: Looking at culture in business is a critical component of long-term success, especially when considering transitions like succession planning. Every company has a culture, and a good one boost profitability, reduces turnover and fosters innovation. By treating culture as a strategic asset rather than an afterthought, businesses can create a "Line of Sight" where every associate understands their personal impact on the company's valuation and long-term future. Even if your business right now is only you, customers and clients will appreciate your vision for how you want your legacy to look. In her role as Executive Director for the Georgia Center for Employee Ownership (GACEO), Marie Davis is responsible for establishing the network of partners and delivery of programming necessary for the GACEO to carry out its mission of the creation of new employee-owned companies throughout all of Georgia. By providing unbiased education, outreach, and technical assistance on all forms of employee ownership, business owners can objectively research succession planning options. In addition to her passion for employee ownership, Marie is also passionate about providing opportunities for all childr
